name: JavaScript performance (hot paths)
description: LOW-MEDIUM: micro-optimizations for hot paths (DOM/layout thrash avoidance, maps/sets for lookups, caching storage calls, combine iterations, early exits, toSorted immutability, requestIdleCallback).

metadata.intent: Improve JS hot-path performance in React apps without creating correctness bugs

must_use: Prefer algorithmic wins (O(n)→O(1)) before micro tweaks
must_not: Introduce caching without invalidation when external changes are possible
conventions: Keep perf changes localized and testable; avoid premature optimization.

steps.description: Avoid interleaving layout reads (`getBoundingClientRect/offsetWidth`) with style writes; batch writes then read once.
Use Map/Set for repeated lookups instead of repeated `.find()`/`.includes()` over arrays.
Cache `localStorage/sessionStorage/cookie` reads in memory; invalidate on `storage` / visibility changes when needed.
Combine multiple filter/map passes into a single loop when it’s a hot path.
Return early when outcome is known; check cheap conditions like array length before expensive comparisons.
Prefer `toSorted()` (or `[...arr].sort`) over mutating `.sort()` to avoid React state/prop mutation bugs.
Defer non-critical work (analytics, prefetch, persistence) using `requestIdleCallback` (with timeout/fallback).
